new_text: [chainovi/eclipse-agave #12] CORRECTED AUDIT: Fee distribution runtime wiring (distribute_transaction_fee_details, update_base_fee_for_new_block, record_transaction_compute) was already active. ACTUAL gap: fee ENFORCEMENT. Bank::calculate_fee() used static lamports_per_signature instead of dynamic base_fee_per_cu. Session wired EIP-1559 enforcement into calculate_fee() and get_fee_for_message_with_lamports_per_signature(). Now when projectdawn_enabled, fee = base_fee_per_cu * compute_unit_limit. All 37 ProjectDawn tests pass.

[chainovi/eclipse-agave #8] ProjectDawn Phase 5 integration tests (12 end-to-end tests, runtime/tests/projectdawn_integration.rs, 2026-03-03). Coverage: developer fee attribution, fallback to treasury, fee market base fee EIP-1559 adjustments, state persistence across child banks, fee split interpolation, emergency unlock markers, tier penalties, epoch rollover, inactive config fallback, multi-program splits. Key pattern: PDA accounts must be planted via genesis_config.accounts BEFORE bank creation. Added pub getters: fee_market_base_fee(), get_fee_market_state(), is_emergency_unlocked().

[chainovi/eclipse-agave] Tests 14-15 in runtime/src/bank/serde_snapshot.rs: Test 14 (projectdawn_config_snapshot_round_trip) creates genesis with projectdawn feature account, injects 12M gas, serializes/deserializes bank. Asserts ProjectDawnConfig, FeeMarketConfig, BlockFeeState survive round-trip; projectdawn_enabled=true after deserialization. Test 15 (projectdawn_disabled_after_snapshot_without_feature) verifies feature disabled state. All 4 test cases (2 tests × 2 storage modes) pass.

GENESIS Plan (Session 343): Custom meta-analysis system using existing 4-layer memory + 50 analytics tools. 5 phases: COLLECT (parallel analytics), CORRELATE (cross-reference patterns), DERIVE (confidence-scored rules), DIGEST (output to genesis/digest-YYYY-MM-DD.md), DIFF (compare vs prior runs). Files: genesis_engine.py, genesis_collector.py, skills/genesis/SKILL.md. Reuses cluster_knowledge, suggest_promotions, memory_health_report. Does NOT auto-modify CLAUDE.md. Plan: plans/peppy-humming-twilight.md.

Auto-Capture System (Option 5 Full Hybrid) IMPLEMENTED — Session 18 (2026-02-12). Files created: secrets_filter.py (8 pattern categories), observation.py (compress_observation), user_prompt_capture.py. Files modified: enforcer.py, memory_server.py, boot.py, settings.json, test_framework.py. Architecture: Enforcer PostToolUse + UserPromptSubmit append compressed observations to .capture_queue.jsonl. MCP server flushes to separate ChromaDB observations collection on search/boot. 3 new MCP tools: search_observations(), get_observation(), timeline(). 30-day TTL, 5K cap, digest generation, crash recovery. 271 tests pass.

Framework Upgrade Proposals (Session 16, 2026-02-12). Proposal 1: Progressive Disclosure — IMPLEMENTED. search_knowledge returns summaries (120-char preview), get_memory(id) for full. 85.5% token savings. Proposal 2: Automatic Capture — NOT YET IMPLEMENTED. Would passively record tool calls, diffs, outputs without manual remember_this(). Blockers: dual-writer DB corruption, search quality degradation, secrets leakage. Required: separate observation collection, secrets filtering, compaction strategy (30-day TTL + 5K cap), relevance scoring. User wants design discussion in next session.

Session 20: Verified 3 items from session 19 ChromaDB fix. (1) timeline() MCP tool: returns float session_time, ±10min window works. (2) Compaction: inserted 5 old obs (45 days), confirmed deletion and digest creation with stats (5 obs, 1 session, 0% error). (3) Regression tests: 4 new tests for float session_time round-trip and digest creation. Total: 271 tests passed, 0 failed.

AUTO-CAPTURE PLAN — Option 5 Full Hybrid (2026-02-12). Queue-based architecture: Enforcer + UserPromptSubmit append compressed observations to .capture_queue.jsonl (~1-2ms). MCP server flushes to separate ChromaDB observations collection on search/boot. Features: Bash/Edit/Write/NotebookEdit/UserPrompt capture, secrets scrubbing (8 patterns), retrieval (search/get/timeline), auto-surface in query_fix_history, 30-day TTL + 5K cap, digest generation, crash recovery. Files: 3 new + 5 modified (~717 lines, ~25 tests). Config: OBSERVATION_TTL_DAYS=30, MAX_OBSERVATIONS=5000, MAX_QUEUE_LINES=500. Plan: plans/streamed-skipping-frost.md.

Claude Code 2.1.41-2.1.50 Feature Audit for Torus (Feb 2026). ACTIVELY USED: 3 LSP plugins (pyright, rust-analyzer, typescript), statusLine command, contextCompactionThreshold, SubagentStart/Stop hooks, LSP_TOOL=1, CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS=64000, plugins marketplace. NOT USED: WorktreeCreate/Remove, ConfigChange, background: true, isolation: worktree, keybindings, defer_loading, disableAllHooks. IRRELEVANT: Ctrl+F kill agents (no background defined), managed hooks hierarchy.

[torus-web #12] Bug audit: 12 bugs identified (11 confirmed). Critical: (1) app/api/users/route.ts exposes encrypted API key, (2) PATCH auth missing. High: (3) steam-auth.ts:193 infinite recursion on 429, (4) admin/rewards mass-completes all pending when no IDs, (5) AdminLog missing enum cases (logs fail silently). Medium: (6) admin-logger missing news cases, (7) steam callback missing .toLowerCase(), (8) parseInt NaN-vulnerable, (9) rankings inconsistent steamId exposure, (10) ai-gateway CSRF returns 401 not 403, (11) referrals/route public readable. Low: (12) resize listener memory leak. Not yet fixed.

Anthropic Code Modernization Playbook (Feb 2026) — ebook/playbook at resources.anthropic.com/code-modernization-playbook. Covers: COBOL → Java/Python migration, prompt engineering for migration/modernization, using Claude Code to analyze legacy codebases, generate tests, safeguard logic. ROI analysis, timelines, organizational readiness. Market impact: IBM stock dropped 13% (worst day since 2000), ~25% for Feb 2026. Anthropic positioned Claude Code as replacement for expensive consulting. COBOL runs ~95% of US ATM transactions.

Idea: Lean on git log as ground truth to combat LIVE_STATE.json drift. Current problem: what_was_done hallucinations. Proposed: Hybrid approach — keep narrative but inject git log (git log -5 --oneline, git diff --stat HEAD~5, git status --short) in boot hook for cross-reference. Field mapping: what_was_done → git log (truth), feature → git branch (truth), uncommitted work → git status + diff (truth), known_issues/next_steps → keep as-is. Trade-off: lose narrative, gain drift-proof truth. Context: inspired by AKIRA killing LIVE_STATE vs our approach (killed HANDOFF.md).

[trading-framework] engine/executor.py created: Executor class (connector, gate_runner, portfolio, journal, notifier). execute(signal) converts signal to order, builds MarketData snapshot (prices, is_market_open), runs gate_runner, notifies if blocked, else submits order, journals trade, returns Order. Auto-sizing: strength * 0.05 * portfolio.total_value / price. engine/core.py created: Engine class (config, strategies, gate_runner, portfolio, journal). Creates Executor internally. run() creates CronScheduler from config (symbols, schedule_interval_seconds, candle_interval), wraps in KeyboardInterrupt. stop() calls scheduler.stop(). _on_candle catches exceptions, skips None/HOLD. Both verified importing OK.

Trading framework timeframe decision: Support all timeframes via scheduler abstraction. Strategy has on_candle()/on_tick() interface, doesn't know timeframe. Scheduler decides WHEN to call. Three scheduler types: Phase 1 (CronScheduler + REST daily/swing), Phase 2 (IntervalScheduler + REST intraday), Phase 3 (StreamScheduler + WebSocket scalping). "All timeframes" means engine supports all but different strategies per timeframe (trends ≠ noise ≠ microstructure). Share same interface. Drawbacks minimal if built in phases. Data: REST polling for daily/intraday, WebSocket for streaming/scalping. Total: ~8 hours across phases.

[trading-framework Session 11] GitHub Release + README (2026-02-09): Created Release v0.1.0-eclipse at project-dawn-l2-chain/agave-empty, tag on projectdawn-l2 branch, author OZmasterAI. 4 binaries uploaded (agave-validator 73MB, solana 35MB, solana-genesis 28MB, solana-keygen 2.7MB), all Linux x86-64 version 2.2.0. README.md written and pushed (commit 4734adc73d) covering overview, features, building, testnet, tests, structure, config, limitations. GitHub auth: OZmasterAI (admin permissions), both tokens showed invalid but API calls worked (rate-limited briefly).
